description: Can you solidify the use of oauth in this product and make it a requirement?

plan.summary: Enforce OAuth as the sole authentication mechanism across the product, removing or blocking any non-OAuth login paths.
acceptance_criteria: All authentication flows use OAuth exclusively; no username/password or other non-OAuth methods are accessible
Any previously supported non-OAuth login endpoints return an appropriate error (e.g. 401 or 410) or are removed entirely
OAuth provider configuration (client ID, scopes, redirect URIs) is documented and stored securely in environment config, not hardcoded
Unauthenticated requests to protected routes are redirected to the OAuth login flow
Existing sessions created before this change are invalidated and users are prompted to re-authenticate via OAuth
Test coverage exists for both successful OAuth login and rejection of non-OAuth attempts
Which OAuth provider(s)? (Google, GitHub, internal IdP, etc.)
Authorization Code flow, PKCE, or client credentials — which is in scope?
Are there service-to-service calls that also need OAuth, or is this user-facing auth only?
Is there a deadline driven by a security audit or compliance requirement?
